text: A cup taught a child how to apologize. It could not speak, but it tilted toward each mistake, held the silence carefully, and returned the room to kindness.
The chair learned to remember tired people. Whenever someone sat down, it offered an older version of their courage and kept their fear from reaching the floor.
A bottle began guiding the river upstream. It pointed with its glass shoulder, persuaded the current to pause, and carried one lost fish back to the rain.
The blanket listened for nightmares and moved before anyone woke. It folded itself into a small shelter, warmed the dark, and promised the room it would keep watch.
A ladder decided which windows deserved the sky. It lifted the smallest house first, lowered the moon to the sill, and taught the roof how to choose.
